dashboard.scriptRouting.azerty.note: <0>Where AZERTY comes from.</0> AZERTY is the third §9 routing group but is <1>not</1> derivable from the script — every Latin script above starts as <2>qwerty-qwertz</2>. AZERTY is a base-layout refinement resolved <3>after</3> the author picks a base, from that base’s structural shape (see <4>lib/scriptAxes.ts</4> — <5>RoutingGroup</5> vs <6>ScriptRoutingGroup</6>). That is why it has no row here.

editor.trackOneIdentity.keyboardIdHint: 1–255 chars; no spaces, parens, brackets, or commas. Used as the zip filename.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.group.body: A group is a batch of rules from the original keyboard that didn't match any recognized pattern. Each tile below pairs the keys you press with the character they produce. Remove the whole group, or click one tile to drop just that mapping.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.groupDescrip: A group is a block of key rules from the original keyboard that hasn't been recognized as a named pattern. The tiles below show rules with visible character output. The group may also contain store-dependent rules that don't appear as tiles; those are shown in the relevant stores' "Used by" panels. Removing this group removes all of it.
dashboard.flowMap.description: A live map of the survey questions, where each branch goes, and the strategy decision tree. It rebuilds from source — change a flow’s question order or a selector rule, rebuild, and this updates.
output.status.touchStale: A mechanics change after the Touch step means the on-screen (touch) keyboard layout is now out of date. Return to the Touch step and re-complete it before downloading or submitting — otherwise the shipped keyboard would include a stale touch layout.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.pattern.body: A pattern is a set of related rules the tool recognized and grouped together, like the rules for typing accented letters. Each tile below pairs the keys you press with the character they produce. Remove the whole pattern, or click one tile to drop just that mapping.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.patternDescr: A recognized pattern groups related key rules by purpose, for example "vowels with diacritics" or "base alphabet". The tiles below show rules with visible character output. The pattern may also own store-dependent rules that don't appear as tiles; those are shown in the relevant stores' "Used by" panels. Removing this pattern removes all of it.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.raw.body: A rule too complex for the editor to show or rewrite. These often look like junk but usually do real work. Leave it unless you're certain this behaviour is unused by your language.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.store.input.b: A set of characters that some of this keyboard's rules depend on as you type. Removing it stops those rules from working. Safe to drop only if your language never uses these characters.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.store.inputOu: A set of characters that some rules depend on as you type and others use to produce output. Removing it affects rules on both sides, so drop it only if your language needs neither.
A set of characters the keyboard can produce as output. Remove it and the rules that insert these characters stop producing them. Safe to drop if your language doesn't need that output.

dashboard.strategyTree.postfixNote: Note: rule 3a (postfix-preference intercept → S-03, shown above between rules 3 and 4) is implemented in <0>selectStrategy()</0>. When a base is instantiated (either track), its IR is scanned for the unconditional postfix sequence-replace shape and <1>markInputOrder="postfix"</1> is seeded onto <2>irAxes</2> (<3>source: "import-derived"</3> below), so rule 3a now fires live for such a base — though <4>if()</4>-guarded rules are still opaque, so no shipping <5>sil_ipa</5> rule reaches it yet. No survey phase elicits it end-to-end yet — that half remains deferred.

dashboard.strategyTree.intro: The survey computes a seven-axis vector (A1–A7), then <0>selectStrategy()</0> runs this tree. Primary rules are tried top-to-bottom; the first match fixes the primary strategy. The secondary passes then always run, appending add-ons.
dashboard.scriptRouting.description: The target script the author picks — not the language — decides the layout family (§9). Latin-family alphabetic scripts route to <0>qwerty-qwertz</0>; everything else routes to <1>non-roman</1>, which then fans out inside Phase B (see the Survey flow tab: <2>pb_non_roman_branch</2> → Indic / SE-Asian / RTL / syllabic / alpha-nonlatin).
output.submit.outputBlockedReason.touchS: the touch layout is out of date — return to the Touch step and re-complete it
editor.touchSeed.importAdaptUnusable: There is no base touch layout to import — this option starts from an empty layout.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.store.referen: These characters are used by some of the keyboard's rules. Removing them may change how those rules behave.
These characters aren't used by any rule in the keyboard. Removing them is safe and won't change how it behaves.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.store.pattern: These characters belong to the "{patternLabel}" pattern. To change or remove them, work through that pattern rather than editing this list directly.
editor.assignLoop.touch.intro.bullet3: These choices apply to touch only and never change your desktop layout.
dashboard.strategyTree.prefilledAxes.des: These phase-gated axes were not elicited by the survey. Each was supplied either by the §7.2 script-class prior (the unmarked/off-state value, never rule-triggering) or derived from structural evidence in the base keyboard (which <0>can</0> be rule-triggering, e.g. <1>markInputOrder="postfix"</1> → rule 3a) — the source is shown on each row. Filled before <2>selectStrategy()</2> ran. Read-only for now; confirm/override UI is a follow-up.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.usedBy.outpu: These rules pick their output character from this list based on which key you pressed.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.usedBy.both: These rules scan your input against this list and also pick their output character from it.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.usedBy.input: These rules scan your input against this list; when you type a matching character, the rule fires.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.pairedStores: These stores work together as a set. Removing one without the others will break the mechanism.
These two stores work as a pair. Removing one without the other will break the mechanism.
editor.touchSeed.absentNote: This base ships no touch layout.
editor.touchSeed.malformedNote: This base's touch layout could not be read (malformed JSON) — treated as no layout.
editor.carve.webPopup.body: This character also lives in these places — click one to jump to it in the rail.
editor.carve.cascade.removeMessage: This character appears in multiple places. Removing it everywhere keeps the keyboard consistent; removing it from just one place may leave broken references.
editor.carve.cascade.cannotFullyRemoveMe: This character is produced by advanced rules that can't be removed automatically — see below.
editor.carve.cascade.restoreMessage: This character was removed from several places. Restore it everywhere it was removed?
editor.touchSeed.intro: This choice seeds the mobile/touch layout. Individual characters can still be reviewed and adjusted afterward in the Touch Layout step.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.storePair.in: This is the input side of a paired-store rule. Its characters line up one-for-one with {pairedList}. When one of these is matched and the rule fires, the keyboard outputs the character at the same position in {pairedList}.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.storePair.ou: This is the output side of a paired-store rule. Each character lines up one-for-one with {pairedList}; the rule picks the matching one based on what was input.
editor.assignLoop.intro.body: This is where you build your keyboard. For each character your language needs that the base layout doesn’t already have, you’ll choose how to type it on a physical (desktop) keyboard.
editor.assignLoop.infoView.overview.body: This keyboard came with rules for typing many different characters. Carving lets you keep only the ones your language needs. Pick an item on the left to see what it does and whether it's safe to remove.
editor.carve.rulesLookGoodBody: This keyboard uses standard rules in a single group — there's nothing complex to review or remove.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.storePair.fa: This list is paired with {pairedList} by position — the lists line up one-for-one.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.storePair.bo: This list sits on both sides of a paired-store rule with {pairedList}: each character is matched as input and re-emitted as output at the same position — a match-and-reproduce, not a one-way input→output swap. The lists still line up one-for-one by position.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.relationship: This panel shows every rule group that depends on this store. Stores are shared character lists. Removing one while rules still reference it will break those rules at compile time. Use this section to understand what's connected before you remove anything.
dashboard.flowGraph.alsoLive.title: This question also appears in a live flow (also live).
step.unsupported.fallback.detail: This script is not yet supported in v1. Please start over and choose a different script, or check back in a future release.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.emptyStoreWa: This will empty the store — the mechanism depending on it will stop working
survey.marks.stacking.tickCombinationsIn: Tick each combination your language actually uses (only ticked combinations will be typable):
survey.marks.attachment.rowHelpText: Tick every letter this mark can sit on. Letters left unticked will not take this mark on your finished keyboard.
editor.assignLoop.inspector.emptyStoreWa: To keep the keyboard buildable, the built keyboard keeps this store's characters until at least one stays active — remove the whole store instead if you no longer need it.
output.submit.error.rateLimit: Too many submissions — please retry in {retryAfterSeconds} seconds.
metadata.tryTyping.heading: Try typing
editor.assignLoop.customInputHelp: Type a character directly, or a Unicode value like U+00E9.
editor.assignLoop.charBoxHelp: Type a character, or a Unicode value like U+00E9. Combine composed parts with spaces, e.g. U+006E U+0303.
editor.assignLoop.method.sequence.title: Type a sequence
survey.phaseB.buildList.typeAlphabetHelp: Type the rest of your alphabet here, putting a space between each character (for example: a b c ŋ ɛ), then press Enter or + Add.
base.picker.search.placeholder: Type to search by name, id, script, or language…
survey.phaseB.charChipEditor.placeholder: Type your alphabet with a space between each character (a b c …)
survey.questionField.autocomplete.namePl: Type your language name in English…
editor.assignLoop.infoView.capabilityHin: Uses advanced syntax the editor can't rewrite, so removing it here won't take effect.
preview.overlay.error.vfs.title: VFS load failed
oauth.callback.error.detail: We couldn’t complete the sign-in. This is usually temporary — please return to the studio and try connecting again.
welcome.title: Welcome to Keyboard Studio
editor.assignLoop.intro.title: Welcome to the Mechanism Gallery
editor.assignLoop.touch.intro.title: Welcome to the Touch Gallery
survey.marks.outputForm.backspacePreview: What backspace does, one press at a time:
editor.carve.webPopup.title: Where "{char}" also appears
survey.marks.attachment.heading: Which letters take each mark?
survey.marks.stacking.allowedOption.yes: Yes — some letters carry two marks
editor.trackStep.intro: You chose <0>{0}</0>. Pick how you want to work with it.
editor.assignLoop.intro.bullet1: You’ll go character by character through the list from your survey.
editor.assignLoop.touch.intro.bullet1: You’ll go character by character, just like the desktop gallery.
survey.marks.stacking.intro: Your alphabet shows letters that may carry more than one mark. Confirm whether the keyboard should allow that, and exactly which combinations.
survey.phaseB.suggestionPanel.baseAlread: Your base keyboard already covers this language's alphabet.
editor.assignLoop.touch.intro.body: Your desktop layout is locked in. Now you’ll set how each character is reached on phones and tablets, where there is no physical keyboard.
output.identity.warn: Your keyboard id is still set to the base keyboard’s id. Downloading now will name the .zip and its internal file paths after the base id. Set your own keyboard name and id before downloading or submitting to the community repository.
output.submit.name.label: Your name (for attribution)
output.submit.success.heading: Your submission is being reviewed.
